title: ‘Star Trek’ 60th Anniversary Celebration, Stars Set for Italian Global Series’ Second Edition
full_text: × Skip to main content By Georg Szalai Plus Icon Georg Szalai Global Business Editor View All June 8, 2026 4:00am 'The Widow Killer' Courtesy of Zeitgeist Filmproduktion/EPO-Film/ARD Degeto/NDR/ServusTV/Canal+/HR/Petro Domenigg The Italian Global Series (IGS) festival is returning to two coastal cities in Italy next month, with a set of special celebrations and a range of stars, writers, actors, producers and other creatives from the world of series. A celebration of the 60th anniversary of Star Trek will be among this year’s highlights. Meanwhile, Robert Powell will celebrate the 50th anniversary of Franco Zeffirelli’s Jesus of Nazareth , a landmark in TV history that was originally intended to be written and directed by Ingmar Bergman. And IGS will celebrate the 30th anniversary of Un posto al sole , Italy’s longest-running soap opera, with the series’ cast presenting and discussing an episode with actress, director and stand-up comedian Michela Giraud, who is known as a fan of the series. Related Stories Movies Maggie Gyllenhaal, Jesse Eisenberg to Receive Karlovy Vary Honors Movies Tamil Blockbuster 'Karuppu' Set for Global Streaming Launch on Amazon Prime Video Among the stars confirmed to attend this year’s festival are British actors John Hannah ( Benidorm Is Murder , The Mummy ) and Bertie Carvel ( A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms , the upcoming Harry Potter series ). The fest, under the artistic direction of Marco Spagnoli, unveiled the majority of its program on Monday, including its International Competition selections, Conversations series and notable Italian premieres. Free to the general public, the series showcase takes place in both Rimini, from July 3 to July 6, and Riccione, from July 7 to July 10, before returning to Rimini on Saturday, July 11 for the closing night ceremony, during which the winners of the competitive sections will be unveiled. IGS said it received more than 160 submissions from around the world this year, with the final selection of the International Competition boasting 21 series from 15 countries, including from Europe, Asia, North America and Latin America. The International Competition, comprised of a drama, a comedy and a limited series section, will see the Maximo Awards awarded for best series, best lead actor, best lead actress and best creator/director, along with a special jury prize. U.S. producer Marti Noxon ( Buffy the Vampire Slayer , Prison Break , Grey’s Anatomy , Mad Men ) will serve as president of the drama series jury, director and screenwriter Nicholas Meyer will be the president of the limited series jury, and French actor and playwright Bruno Gouery (Emily in Paris , The White Lotus ) will serve as the president of the comedy series jury. The slate of conversations at IGS this year includes Carlton Cuse hosting a session entitled “Lost in Fellini,” for which he will be joined by Oscar-winning writer and showrunner John Ridley ( American Crime , 12 Years a Slave ), a conversation between executive producer David W. Zucker ( Alien: Earth , Dope Thief , Blade Runner 2099 ) and producer Steve Stark ( The Handmaid’s Tale , Wednesday , Fargo ) about the future of international production and an event with married Turkish film and social media stars Ozge Gürel and Serkan Çayoğlu, in which they will discuss their work and their lives in the public eye. Among the other sessions will be a conversation with actress Jeri Ryan, known for her role in the Star Trek universe, about contemporary female role models in TV and in science fiction in particular, plus a chat between Zucker and Nicholas Meyer, director of several Star Trek films. The festival will also host a special preview screening of an episode of S tar Trek: Strange New Worlds . The fourth season of the series will debut, following the event, on July 23, on Paramount+. Check out the International Competition lineup for the second edition of IGS below. International Competition Drama Section Benidorm Is Murder , a U.K./Spanish production directed by Simon Delaney and starring John Hannah; Emergency 53 , an adrenaline-fueled medical series from Brazil Secret Service , a political spy thriller directed by Oscar winner James Marsh and starring Gemma Arterton Speaking Dead , a Korean thriller Burden of Justice , a Swedish legal drama I casi di Teresa Battaglia – Figlia della cenere , an Italian drama by Kiko Rosati that stars Elena Sofia Ricci The Terror With Devil in Silver , an anthology series produced by Scott Free and the first U.S. title in the competition program of the festival, with appearances set for Riccione by its two leads, Judith Light and CCH Pounder Limited Series Section Le Rouge et Le Noir , a new adaptation of Stendhal’s novel The Red and the Black , starring Victor Belmondo and featuring Camille Razat and Virginie Ledoyen, both of whom will be travelling to Rimini alongside director Gaël Morel Falling , the first romance by Jack Thorne and directed by Peter Hoar The Widow Killer , a noir set in Prague in 1945 Raza Brava , an epic tale of rebellion set in the world of Santiago’s ultras, beginning during the Chilean General Augusto Pinochet dictatorship Laura’s Treatment , starring Valérie Bonneton, from France One of Us by Andrea Rebuzzi, starring Raoul Bova The Tribute , a twist-filled family drama starring Elsa Pataky, with a cameo by her husband , Chris Hemsworth Comedy Section Boho , a generational portrait set in the multicultural neighborhood of Borgerhout in Belgium The Flaws, a German comedy created by a theater collective and produced by Razor Film Happily Ever After from Canada, which challenges the myth of “forever” at age 25 Una piccola formalità , an Italian series by Davide Marengo and starring Pilar Fogliati Leonard and Hungry Paul , an Irish comedy starring Alex Lawther and featuring the voice of Julia Roberts Riot Women from the U.K., which bears the signature of creator Sally Wainwright, who will be attending the festival alongside star Tamsin Greig Cell Block B , a prison comedy originating from the Czech Republic THR

title: John Waters, Billy Porter and Julio Torres Set For Second Season of ‘Finding Fire Island’ Series
full_text: × Skip to main content By Chris Gardner Plus Icon Chris Gardner chrissgardner Follow Sign Up View All June 8, 2026 4:00am Billy Porter, John Waters, Julio Torres, Cody Rigsby, Jay Jurden Leon Bennett/Getty Images; Dominik Bindl/Getty Images; Manoli Figetakis/Getty Images; Arturo Holmes/Getty Images; Jerod Harris/Getty Images Finding Fire Island has found its way back. The GLAAD Award nominated documentary podcast series is set to debut a second season on June 11. The new batch of episodes focuses on the Pines, a section of the island notable for its parties and exclusivity. But per the team, the second season will dive behind the music and lore to take a closer look at the community that helps “keep the fantasy alive.” In doing so, series creator Jess Rothschild, who executive produces and narrates the episodes, snagged a list of notable names to help tell the story, including filmmaker John Waters , Emmy winner Billy Porter , comedian, writer, performer and actor Julio Torres , deejay Lina Bradford, Tony-winning Broadway producer and choreographer Jerry Mitchell, Peloton breakout star Cody Rigsby, comedian Jay Jurden, and Fire Island deejays Susan Morabito and Robbie Leslie. The first season of Finding Fire Island featured Margaret Cho, Joel Kim Booster, Matt Rogers and others. In addition to the names, Finding Fire Island , which is co-produced with the Pines Historical Society, features rare archival footage. She previously told The Hollywood Reporter that Fire Island was “intrinsically formative” to her identity as a gay person growing up in New York. “As my obsession with the history and culture of Fire Island intensified over the years, I began collecting interviews with notable Fire Island figures, past and present,” Rothschild explained of the gay mecca. “This new season has allowed me to delve into stories that have yet to be told.” The first two episodes of season two drop June 11, with a weekly drop from there. The series is distributed via the Broadway Podcast Network and available on SiriusXM, YouTube, Spotify, Apple Podcasts and Amazon Music. As for Rothschild, she’s also hosted Hot Takes & Deep Dives and created the docuseries, Cult of Body & Soul , about all things SoulCycle. THR

Jury members include Amanda Righetti ( Scarpetta ), Maurizio Lombardi ( Citadel: Diana ), Katia Fellin ( Weiss and Morales ), Tom Wlaschiha ( Game of Thrones ), Francesca Inaudi ( The Hunter ), Barbara Chichiarelli ( Mussolini: Son of the Century ), Assaad Bouab ( Bad Sisters ), Victor Alli ( Bridgerton ), singer Kaze, Italian-Canadian executive Rudy Buttignol, composer Pivio, director Lisa Mulcahy ( The Assassin ) and producer Pier Giorgio Bellocchio ( De Generazione ). Elsewhere, Carlton Cuse will host a panel titled Lost in Fellini. He will be joined by John Ridley ( American Crime ). David W. Zucker ( Alien: Earth ) and producer Steve Stark ( The Handmaid’s Tale ) will host a session on the future of international productions. Italian Global Series is directed by Marco Spagnoli and conceived and organized by APA (the Association of Audiovisual Producers), in collaboration with Cinecittà, with the support of the Ministry of Culture, the Emilia-Romagna Region, the municipalities of Riccione and Rimini, and AGIS. IGS 2026 – International Competition DRAMA BENIDORM IS MURDER – UK / Spain – World Premiere Director Simon Delaney Written by Ian Jarvis Cast John Hannah, Carolina Bécquer Production Blackbox Multimedia and Clapperboard Studios, ZDF Distribution ZDF A light-hearted crime drama set in a holiday resort on Spain’s Costa Blanca, where a reluctant and irresistibly laid-back detective solves murders under the Mediterranean sun. He is played by the versatile Scottish actor John Hannah, beloved by comedy audiences for roles ranging from Four Weddings and a Funeral to The Mummy franchise . Dennis and Rosa are a crime-solving duo: a burned-out ex-cop running a shabby pub and a wannabe actress. They solve murders in hotels, clubs, caravan parks and on the beach, juggling sunburnt tourists, feuding families and missing cash while Inspector Maria and assistant Jesús watch them somehow crack the cases. BURDEN OF JUSTICE – Sweden – Italian Premiere Original Title Hundarna Director Lisa Linnertorp Written by Frans Milisic Wiklund, Jens Lapidus Cast Björn Bengtsson, Arvin Kananian, Kit Walker Johansson, Tiril Wishman Production Strive Stories Distribution DR Sales An intensely gripping legal drama, driven by a relentless pace, morally ambiguous characters, and an aggressive visual style that makes extensive use of handheld camerawork. Winner of the Audience Award at the Series Mania Festival in Lille, it was a major success in its home country and has already been renewed for a second season. Burden of Justice peels back the polished surface of Stockholm’s elite law firms to reveal a world where desperation, ambition, and moral compromise simmer just beneath the surface. Set in a justice system under pressure, the series delivers an exploration of truth, loyalty, and power — where every verdict has a price. I CASI DI TERESA BATTAGLIA – FIGLIA DELLA CENERE Italy – World Premiere The Teresa Battaglia Cases – Daughter of Ash Director Kiko Rosati Written by Mario Cristiani, Donatella Diamanti, Valerio D’Annunzio, Vittoria Benedetti, based on the novel by Ilaria Tuti Cast Elena Sofia Ricci, Gianluca Gobbi, Giuseppe Spata, Fausto Maria Sciarappa, Sara Drago Production Rai Fiction, Publispei, with the contribution of the Friuli Venezia Giulia Film Commission – PromoTurismoFVG Distribution Rai Created by acclaimed author Ilaria Tuti, Teresa Battaglia is brought to the screen by Elena Sofia Ricci in one of the most beloved crime series in contemporary Italian television. Teresa Battaglia returns in a compelling new chapter. In this third season, the profiler faces one of the most complex cases of her career: a serial killer with a dark past who leaves disturbing clues and ritual mutilations along a mysterious symbolic trail. Alongside the investigation, Teresa’s personal life is shaken by the return of a ghost from her past, in a story woven with tension, emotion, and revelations. Against a backdrop of historic sites and urban landscapes, the team moves among Udine, Aquileia, and Cividale del Friuli in a narrative in which the line between justice and truth becomes increasingly blurred. EMERGENCY 53 – Brasil – World Premiere Original Title Emergência 53 Director Claudio Torres, Andrucha Waddington Written by Claudio Torres, Fabio Mendes Cast Yara de Novaes, Valentina Herszage, Emilio Dantas, Heloísa Jorge, Ana Hikari, Raquel Villar, William Nascimento, Jaffar Bambirra, Emilio de Mello, Fernanda Montenegro Production Globoplay Distribution Globo Presented at the Berlinale Series Market, Emergency 53 is being screened before an audience for the first time. An adrenaline-fueled medical drama, it follows paramedics racing against the clock through the streets and favelas of Rio de Janeiro, with a visual style openly inspired by American superhero imagery. Dr. Irene leads a mobile emergency unit with two ambulances, each staffed by doctors, nurses, and drivers. Skilled yet overlooked, they operate between accident scenes and overcrowded hospitals, navigating daily chaos while saving lives. Through their stories, we witness the intense reality of those who live to keep others alive. SECRET SERVICE – UK – Italian Premiere Director James Marsh Written by Tom Bradby, Jemma Kennedy Cast Gemma Arterton, Rafe Spall, Mark Stanley, Alex Kingston, Roger Allam, Amaka Okafor, Khalid Abdalla Production Potboiler Productions for ITVX, UK in association with All3Media International Distribution All3Media International Directed by Academy Award winner James Marsh ( Man on Wire, The Theory of Everything ), starring Gemma Arterton ( Quantum of Solace, The Boat That Rocked ) and Rafe Spall ( The Big Short, Black Mirror ), and based on Tom Bradby’s trilogy of novels, Secret Service is an elegant and exotic spy thriller, true to the traditions of the genre, yet also deeply political, drawing viewers into a web of moral ambiguity and power struggles. Kate Henderson is a senior member of MI6 running a secret intelligence operation out of Valletta, Malta. Lena, working for Kate, is staying at a villa belonging to a senior member of Russia’s Foreign Intelligence Service, masquerading as his son’s nanny. When Lena places a bug in a strategic location, Kate and her team listen in. They overhear snatches of a conversation saying that the UK’s Prime Minister is due to resign and that the Russians have a candidate in ‘pole position’ to replace him. Shocked, Kate returns to the UK. Her superiors are reluctant to investigate until they have more concrete information, but Kate and her teammate Rav are convinced they need to move quickly. When the Prime Minister calls a surprise press conference and announces he’s standing down, proving the worth of her intelligence, Kate and MI6 go into overdrive. How did the Russians know in advance? And could there really be a mole at the heart of the British government? They begin digging into the key candidates who could be in line for the PM’s replacement, including Ryan Walker, the Foreign Secretary, and Imogen Conrad, the Chancellor. This puts Kate in a difficult position; Imogen is one of Kate’s closest friends and her husband’s boss. With tensions mounting, Kate uncovers more secrets, some closer to home than others, and wonders who she can trust. She also needs to get Lena out of Malta – and fast. As the political contest between Ryan and Imogen hots up, the race is on for Kate and MI6 to work out the extent of Russia’s links and the identity of the mole. SPEAKING DEAD – South Korea – International Premiere Original title Seupiking Dedeu Director Lee Junghyo Written by Cast Han Suk-kyu, Jung Yumi Production Distribution SLL Speaking Dead is an intense thriller built across multiple timelines, starring Han Suk-kyu, one of South Korea’s most acclaimed actors and the winner of last year’s Maximo Award at Italian Global Series in the Drama section for Doubt . A forensic scientist resorts to extreme measures to force the reinvestigation of a prosecutor’s suspicious death. As a profiler retraces the case, hidden motives and buried evidence emerge, revealing a tense investigation that uncovers a deeper network of power and corruption. THE TERROR: DEVIL IN SILVER – USA – Italian Premiere Director Karyn Kusama Written by based on the novel by Victor LaValle, Showrunner Christopher Cantwell Cast Dan Stevens, Judith Light, CCH Pounder, Aasif Mandvi, Stephen Root, John Benjamin Hickey Production Scott Free Productions, Emjag Productions, Entertainment 360 Distribution AMC Networks The Terror is the first American title competing at IGS and marks the eagerly anticipated return of the acclaimed horror anthology. In its third incarnation, the series reconnects with its literary roots through an adaptation of the novel by acclaimed African American author Victor LaValle. A prestigious production from Sir Ridley Scott’s company, it is overseen by Halt and Catch Fire showrunner Christopher Cantwell and features a cast including Dan Stevens ( Downton Abbey ), Judith Light ( Transparent ) and CCH Pounder ( The Shield ). Pepper, a working-class moving man, finds himself wrongfully committed to New Hyde Psychiatric Hospital through a combination of bad luck and a bad temper. Inside the institution — home to those society would rather forget — he must contend with patients working against him, doctors harboring grim secrets, and perhaps even the Devil himself. As Pepper navigates a hellscape where nothing is as it seems, he discovers that the only path to freedom is to confront the entity feeding on the suffering within New Hyde’s walls. But doing so may reveal that the worst demons of all live inside him. LIMITED SERIES FALLING – UK – Italian Premiere Director Peter Hoar Written by Jack Thorne Cast Keeley Hawes, Paapa Essiedu, Rakie Ayola, Jason Watkins, Niamh Cusack, Adrian Scarborough, David Dawson, Susan Brown, Sandra Voe Production The Forge Entertainment (a Banijay UK company) Distribution Banijay Falling marks Jack Thorne’s first venture into romantic storytelling. Widely regarded as one of the most talented screenwriters of his generation, Thorne has explored adolescence, trauma and marginality in works such as Adolescence , Lord of the Flies and Harry Potter and the Cursed Child . Here, the British writer brings his intense and emotionally charged style to a love story. Directing is Peter Hoar, acclaimed for the visual and human sensitivity of series such as It’s a Sin and The Last of Us. Keeley Hawes (Miss Austen, Bodyguard) stars as Anna, a devoted nun, and Paapa Essiedu (Harry Potter, Black Mirror, The Lazarus Project) as David, a Catholic priest, in BAFTA and Emmy award- winning writer Jack Thorne’s first ever love story, directed by BAFTA-winning Peter Hoar (It’s a Sin, Umbrella Academy). Both are deeply committed to their work in the church and their communities. Neither expected to fall in love. But when they do, both are forced to wrestle with what it means for them, their vows, and their relationship with God. Falling is a witty, moving, contemporary romantic drama that is as honest as it is full of heart. LAURA’S TREATMENT – France – Italian Premiere Original Title L’affaire Laura Stern Director Akim Isker Written by Marie Kremer, Fréderik Krivine Cast Valérie Bonneton, Pauline Parigot, Rym Foglia, Samir Guesmi, Pascal Rénéric, Yannick Rénier, Marie-Sophie Ferdane, Eva Huault, Darren Muselet, Catherine Amé. Production Tetra Media Fiction Distribution France Télévision Best Drama Series at the most recent Festival de la Fiction in La Rochelle, Laura’s Treatment stars Valérie Bonneton, a leading figure of French cinema who has worked with directors such as André Téchiné, Patrice Leconte and Guillaume Canet. The series confronts, head-on and without easy answers, themes such as toxic relationships, femicide and patriarchy. For this reason, it will be screened as part of a special “anti-patriarchal” evening alongside Riot Women . Laura, a pharmacist and mother, has devoted her life to helping victims of domestic violence, founding the organization ‘Women Standing Tall.’ But when she witnesses the shocking murder of one of her members, her world is shattered. Tormented by the femicide and outraged by the indifference of the police and justice system, Laura decides to take matters into her own hands. How far will she go to protect the women around her? ONE OF US – Italy – World Premiere Director Andrea Rebuzzi Written by Massimo Bacchini, Elena Tramonti, Sara Cavosi, based on the series Lycée Toulouse-Lautrec created by Fanny Riedberger Cast Raoul Bova, Giulia Bevilacqua, Gloria Harvey, Francesco Leo, Anna Favella, Adriana Savarese, Fiorenza Pieri, Federica Pagliaroli Production Rai Fiction, Fabula Pictures Distribution Rai Italian adaptation of the French series Lycée Toulouse-Lautrec , starring Raoul Bova as the principal of a school where inclusion is not an abstract principle but a daily challenge. Vittoria is a seventeen-year-old girl from an affluent Roman family who is forced to move to a provincial town after her mother enrolls her autistic brother, Matteo, at the Hellen Keller High School, an institution that specializes in providing practical and emotional support to students with disabilities and their families. After initially rejecting the new reality she finds herself in, Vittoria must overcome her prejudices and become part of a close-knit group of friends facing the typical challenges of adolescence, on a journey in which each character confronts their own limitations. RAZA BRAVA – Chile – International Premiere Director Hernán Caffiero Written by Hernán Caffiero, Pavel Piña Cast Gabriel Muñoz, David Gaete, Karla Melo, Daniel Antivilo Production DeCulto Distribution The Media Pro Studio Distribution Among the most acclaimed titles presented at last year’s Berlinale Series Market, Raza Brava is a story of violent rebellion whose roots stretch back to the 1970s, during the Pinochet dictatorship. It offers a social and anthropological look at the most extreme fringes of organized football fandom in South America. After a street fight, Carlos and Clavo forge a friendship on the outskirts of Santiago, shaped by soccer and dictatorship. As adults, they join Colo-Colo’s supporters’ group and enter a world of mafia and violence. After the death of the Garra Blanca’s historic leader, Carlos – now known as Barti – rises as his successor. LE ROUGE ET LE NOIR – France – International Premiere Director Gaël Morel Written by Georges-Marc Benamou – Based on the book by Stendhal. Gilles Taurand, Georges-Marc Benamou Cast Victor Belmondo, Camille Razat, Virginie Ledoyen, Patrick Timsit, Patrick Mille, Romane Bohringer, Théo Cholbi, Zoé Adjani, Sophie Guillemin, Fred Testot Production Siècle Productions Distribution Wild Bunch TV France has not adapted one of its most celebrated novels, Stendhal’s The Red and the Black , for either the small or the big screen since 1998. Today, the web of passions and ambitions that shapes the dazzling life of Julien Sorel returns at the heart of a major production. Rather than seeking to modernize the text through flamboyant stylistic devices, director Gaël Morel offers a nuanced reinterpretation of its human relationships, focusing on how the characters interact, confront one another, and engage in dialogue. The journey of the ambitious and conflicted Julien Sorel, torn between two women as opposite as they are complementary—Louise de Rênal and Mathilde de La Mole—and who will die for it. Based on the work by Stendh THE TRIBUTE – Spain – Italian Premiere Original title El Homenaje Director Sergio Cánovas Written by Sergio Cánovas, Javier Naya, Martín Suárez Cast Elsa Pataky, Eusebio Poncela, Ángela Molina, Luis Tosar, Juana Acosta, Enrique Arce, Miriam Giovanelli, Álvaro Rico, Oscar de la Fuente, Luisa Mayol, Raúl Prieto, Manu Ríos, Georgina Amorós, Lydia Pavón, Lola Alterio. Production Stellarmedia, Secuoya Studios Distribution Secuoya A layered ensemble family thriller where everyone has something to hide, and revelations are revealed, one after another. Moving between past and present, Sergio Cánovas’s new series is a finely crafted and intricately constructed narrative machine that has attracted an impressive roster of stars from Spanish cinema and television. At his 80th birthday party, the powerful patriarch Adolfo Novak gathers family and friends. Behind the smiles lie decades of secrets, betrayals, and revenge. The evening erupts, revealing a truth more shocking than anyone could have ever suspected. THE WIDOW KILLER – Germany / Austria – International Premiere Original title Sternstunde der Mörder Director Christopher Schier Written by Klaus Burck, based on the novel of the same name by Pavel Kohout Cast Nicholas Ofczarek, Jonas Nay, Jeanette Hain, Devid Striesow, Gerhard Liebmann, Diana Dulinková, Karel Dobrý Production Zeitgeist Filmproduktion, Bravado Media GmbH Distribution Bravado Media GmbH Based on Pavel Kohout’s novel The Widow Killer, this German-Austrian period thriller boasts lavish production values and an atmosphere reminiscent of Babylon Berlin and The Third Man , particularly in its evocative use of noir lighting. The two lead roles are played by prominent faces of contemporary German television: Jonas Nay ( Deutschland 83 ) and Nicholas Ofczarek ( Pagan Peak ) . In Nazi-occupied Prague, 1945, Czech inspectors Beran and Morava hunt a ritualistic serial killer alongside suspicious German detective Buback. As the city erupts in uprising, the investigation spirals into a harrowing struggle for justice, shifting from a manhunt to a profound exploration of guilt, revenge and retribution. COMEDY BOHO – Belgium – Italian Premiere Director Olympia Allaert Created by Abbie Boutkabout Written by Abbie Boutkabout, Zita Theunynck, Philippe De Schepper Cast Serine Ayari, Miss Angel, Ikram Aoulad Production jonnydepony Distribution Banijay Awarded at the most recent edition of Canneseries, Boho is a comedy that explores themes of integration and the condition of women in contemporary society. What truly sets it apart, however, is its vibrant and colorful universe, where imagination can suddenly burst onto the screen in musical-like dance sequences. To fully appreciate its themes and atmosphere, the series will be presented with two episodes. The feel-good Streamz Original series “BOHO” tells the story of the friendship between Kima, Nawal, and Alex, three women in their thirties from Borgerhout in Antwerp. Kima is a dreamer with a passion for dance, but her dreams constantly clash with her critical mother’s expectations. Fortunately, her sister Nawal and her friend Alex are there to help her search for happiness, joy, and what she truly wants. Borgerhout is known for its rich mix of traditions and cultures. The neighborhood serves not only as the colorful backdrop but also as the defining factor in this series. The lead roles are played by stand-up comedian Serine Ayari (Exen, Holy Rosita), actress Ikram Aoulad (Arcadia, Geldwolven), and hip-hop artist Miss Angel, who is making her acting debut. CELL UNIT B – Czechia – World Premiere Original Title Director Jan Prušinovský Written by Petr Kolečko, Jan Prušinovský Cast Tadeáš Moravec, Zdeněk Godla, Julius Oračko, Martin Gorol, Marsell Bendig, Nick Ferenc, František Balog, Matěj Hádek, Zuzana Stivínová, Táňa Malíková, Jan Jankovský, Kristýna Jedličková, Adam Ernest, Viktor Tauš, Václav Marhoul, Martin Wox Musil Production Distribution Nova TV Prison comedy and the first Czech series competing at the festival, Cell Block B portrays prison life without sugarcoating and with just the right rough edge. Sometimes the irony turns bitter and, with a typically Eastern European sensibility, highlights the paradoxes of institutions, as well as the madness and meanness born from the pursuit and preservation of power. In ten episodes of this comedy series, viewers will learn how to make a vibrator out of a toothbrush and a crossbow out of a coat hanger alongside young first-time convict David Broulík. They will also learn how to survive an intestinal epidemic, celebrate Easter, and make effective use of a visit without visual and auditory checks. THE FLAWS – Germany – Italian Premiere Original title Das Manko Director Arne Feldhusen Written by Created by Writing Collective Das Manko Gbr, Bastian Reiber, Arne Feldhusen Written by Bastian Reiber, Julia Schubert, Jonas Hien, Sebastian Grünewald, David Simon Cast Bastian Reiber, Julia Schubert, Jonas Hien, David Simon, Sebastian Grünewald, Carol Schuler, Jan Krauter, Annika Meier, Christoph Jöde, Florian Anderer, Sarah Bauerett Production Razor Film in co-production with ZDF Distribution Razor Film Winner at the Seriencamp Festival in Cologne and recipient of the Best Director Award in the International Panorama section at Series Mania in Lille, The Flaws is the most original comedy of the year. Conceived, performed and brilliantly choreographed by the theatre collective Das Manko Gbr, the series tackles the world of work with a distinctly absurdist sensibility, somewhere between Charlie Chaplin’s Modern Times and Jacques Tati’s Playtime. The Flaws are the typical employees of a German government office – quiet, obedient and spectacularly bad at their jobs. Protected by a compassionate boss, they are about to be fired when a group of consultants audits the department—until a lucky mix-up of events reroutes them to an advanced training program. Stranded on an autonomous bus, they embark on an absurd journey straight into professional disasters, including hospital mayhem, a mini-revolution on forklifts, and an ill-advised attempt to enter heaven. HAPPILY EVER AFTER – Canada – International Premiere Original title Ils vécurent heureux Director Sandra Coppola, Juliette Gosselin Written by Guillaume Girard, Audrey Béland, Juliette Gosselin Cast Lévi Doré, Chanel Mings, Joephilip Lafortune, Myriam Gaboury, Robin L’Houmeau, Zeneb Blanchet, Frédéric Millaire-Zouvi, Madeleine Péloquin, Félix-Antoine Tremblay, Philippe Thibeault-Denis Production PassezGo Distribution PassezGo Broadcast in Canada by Crave, the same network behind the queer hit Heated Rivalry, Happily Ever After tells the story of a heterosexual relationship through a narrative rich with queer sensibilities. From the very end of its first episode, the series begins to question the very concept of marriage itself. For this reason, the festival will exceptionally present two episodes. We meet Emma and Leo on their wedding day, convinced that, at 25, they know everything about love and commitment. But can their relationship withstand the urge to challenge convention? LEONARD AND THE HUNGRY PAUL – Ireland – Italian Premiere Director Andrew Chaplin Written by Richie Conroy & Mark B. Hodkinson; Based on the novel by Rónán Hession Cast Alex Lawther, Laurie Kynaston, Jamie-Lee O’Donnell, Julia Roberts (voice) Production Avalon Distribution Distribution Subotica Accompanied by the voice of Julia Roberts as narrator, the series is based on the novel of the same name by Irish writer and musician Rónán Hession. Leonard and Hungry Paul is a delicate coming-of-age story in adulthood, poised between grief, friendship and the discovery of love, seen through the disarming perspective of an outsider. The role finds its ideal interpreter in Alex Lawther, already acclaimed for his unforgettable performance in the Black Mirror episode Shut Up and Dance and in Alien: Earth. The series follows two board-gaming friends in their thirties: Leonard, a ghostwriter of children’s encyclopedias, and Hungry Paul, a part-time postal worker living at home. As they drift through quiet suburban life, Leonard’s mother’s death, Hungry Paul’s sister’s wedding, and a new romance push them to face a world suddenly wider and full of unexpected possibilities. RIOT WOMEN – UK – Italian Premiere Director Sally Wainwright, Amanda Brotchie Written by Sally Wainwright Cast Joanna Scanlan, Rosalie Craig, Tamsin Grieg, Lorraine Ashbourne, Amelia Bullmore Production Drama Republic Distribution Mediawan The raw, rock-and-roll cry of Britain’s riot girls as they reach middle age has struck a chord with UK audiences, earning Riot Women a swift BBC renewal for a second season. Still unreleased in Italy, we are honored to present in competition the new series by the acclaimed Sally Wainwright, winner of four BAFTA Awards and nominated eight more times, already celebrated for cult and queer landmarks such as Happy Valley and Gentleman Jack . Openly feminist in both tone and subject matter, Riot Women will be screened alongside Laura’s Treatment as part of a special evening dedicated to an “anti-patriarchal” perspective. Dive headfirst into the world of five women who, along with two spirited backup singers, come together to create an improvised punk-rock band to compete in a local contest. As they write their first original song, they quickly discover that they have a lot to say – and this is their way of saying it. As they juggle demanding jobs, grown-up children, complicated parents, runaway husbands and disastrous dates, their band becomes the catalyst for change in their lives, leading them to question everything. UNA PICCOLA FORMALITÀ – Italy – World Premiere A Small Formality Director Davide Marengo Written by Eleonora Cimpanelli, Michela Straniero, Vittoria Benedetti, Sebastiano Melloni, Flaminia Gressi, based on the novel by Alessia Gazzola Cast Pilar Fogliati, Alessio Boni, Lorenzo Richelmy, Stefano Rossi Giordani Production Clemart, in collaboration with Rai Fiction Distribution Rai The adaptation of Alessia Gazzola’s novel of the same name, by the author already known for L’Allieva , stars Pilar Fogliati as a young woman caught between a mysterious inheritance, a notary burdened by a complicated past, and the glamorous backdrop of Milan. Blending the tones of romantic comedy with a story of personal emancipation, the series follows a journey of self-discovery, ambition, and unexpected love. Rachele is thirty years old and works on the editorial staff of a lifestyle magazine in Milan, although she has dreamed of becoming an investigative journalist since childhood. When she is unexpectedly named the sole heir of an uncle she never knew, she is drawn into a web of family secrets and reunited with Manfredi, an attractive notary and former high school classmate. All of this happens just as her love life seems ready to take off with her colleague Alessio, the man she has always imagined as her ideal partner. title: Monte-Carlo TV Festival’s 65th Edition: An Inside Take On The Event’s Industry-Fan Formula
full_text: Continue to Deadline SKIP AD You will be redirected back to your article in seconds Skip to main content Dismiss News Banner Breaking News Tony Awards Winners: 'Schmigadoon!' Best Musical, 'Liberation' Best Play; John Lithgow, Lesley Manville, 'Ragtime' Duo Top Acting Honors Read the full story By Stewart Clarke Stewart Clarke More Stories By Stewart ‘The Little Cult That Could’: A Nine-Year Journey Into A Michigan Doomsday Group Next Wave Of European Showrunners Revealed Elisee Junior St Preux On Bringing Haiti To Tribeca With ‘The Tropic Sun And His Eyes’ View All June 8, 2026 2:52am Monte-Carlo TV Festival Monte-Carlo TV Festival The eyes of the sporting world were on Monaco this weekend as Kimi Antonelli sped to victory at the Formula 1 Grand Prix. The principality also regularly provides a glamorous backdrop for movies and events. When it comes to TV, it has been the host of the Monte-Carlo TV Festival since the early 1960s. The Festival blends events for the public and the industry and the 65 th edition will open with Season 3 of The Walking Dead: Dead City , while Kristin Scott Thomas and Kurt Russell will receive its top honor, the Crystal Nymph. Ester Expósito and Matthew Broome , meanwhile, will take accolades for most promising talent, while a raft of projects compete for Golden Nymph awards across fiction, digital, and news and documentary categories. Related Stories News Goldie Hawn & Kate Hudson Have "Thought A Lot About" Making A Movie Together: "Never Say Never" News Monte-Carlo TV Festival To Open With 'The Walking Dead: Dead City' Season 3; Ester Expósito & Matthew Broome Set For Most Promising Talent Honors Ahead of this year’s Festival getting underway, its Executive Director, Cécile Menoni, gave Deadline the lowdown. Watch on Deadline DEADLINE: The calendar of festivals and events is jam packed. Where does the Monte-Carlo TV Festival fit in? Cécile Menoni : Many events focus primarily on the industry, while others are designed mainly for fans and the public. What makes Monte-Carlo unique is its ability to bring together every part of the television ecosystem in a single destination. Over five days, writers, producers, executives, talent, journalists and audiences share the same spaces, attend the same premieres and participate in the same conversations. There is also a strong symbolic dimension to the Festival. Created by Prince Rainier III in 1961, it has accompanied virtually every major transformation in television history, from the era of public broadcasters to cable television, the rise of international co-productions, the streaming revolution and now the emergence of digital-first storytelling. DEADLINE: This will be the 65 th edition of the Festival, what can we expect? Cécile Menoni : From Dame Kristin Scott Thomas and Kurt Russell, who will receive our Crystal Nymphs, to Jeffrey Dean Morgan and Lauren Cohan presenting The Walking Dead: Dead City , the Festival brings together some of the most influential figures in contemporary television. We are also delighted to welcome creators and producers such as Michael Hirst, one of the most respected storytellers in the industry, alongside talents including Aldis Hodge, Ben Watkins and Ester Expósito, recipient of the International Golden Nymph for Most Promising Talent. I am equally proud of the richness of this year’s Official Competition, which showcases programs from around the world and reflects the extraordinary diversity of storytelling today. DEADLINE: H ow do you balance the mix of industry and public events? Cécile Menoni : The public side of the Festival creates excitement, visibility and genuine engagement between viewers and the people who create the programs they love. The industry side creates opportunities for collaboration, networking and strategic discussions. We are not a market in the traditional sense, and that distinction is important. Our objective is not simply to facilitate business transactions, but to create an environment where meaningful conversations, creative exchanges and lasting relationships can develop naturally. Our role is to create an environment where both communities can interact naturally. The Business Forum, premieres, public screenings, talent encounters and industry discussions all contribute to a broader ecosystem that reflects the many ways television brings people together. DEADLINE: How has the Festival evolved? Cécile Menoni : A decade ago, many conversations centered primarily on broadcasters and traditional commissioning models. Today, the landscape is considerably more complex. Global streamers, digital platforms, independent producers, creator-led businesses and emerging technologies are all influencing how stories are developed, financed, distributed and consumed. Our responsibility as a Festival is not simply to reflect those changes but to anticipate them. This is one of the reasons why we have expanded the Business Forum, introduced new industry-focused discussions and launched the Digital Competition. These allow us to recognize excellence across a broader spectrum of content while fostering conversations about the future of storytelling. DEADLINE: Tell us about this year’s Crystal Nymphs and why Dame Kristin and Kurt Russell were selected. Cécile Menoni : The Crystal Nymph honors individuals whose careers have left a lasting mark on the television industry and on audiences worldwide. Dame Kristin Scott Thomas has built an extraordinary career distinguished by elegance, intelligence and artistic excellence. Across television, film and theatre, she has consistently delivered remarkable performances while maintaining a unique creative integrity that has earned admiration across generations. Kurt Russell represents another exceptional journey. Over several decades, he has become one of the most respected and recognizable figures in international entertainment. His versatility, longevity and ability to connect with audiences across generations make him a truly deserving recipient. DEADLINE: The Festival has always attracted Hollywood and U.S. talent. How important has that dimension been? Cécile Menoni : This longstanding connection with American talent and the U.S. entertainment industry has been part of the Festival’s DNA since its creation by Prince Rainier III in 1961 and remains one of the cornerstones of its international identity today. What is particularly fascinating today is how much the relationship between Europe and the United States has evolved. The flow of ideas is no longer one-directional. European series are traveling globally, international co-productions are becoming increasingly common and audiences are embracing stories regardless of their country of origin. DEADLINE: As you look back on your time with the Festival, what are the moments and highlights that come to mind? Cécile Menoni : This anniversary has encouraged us to look back at an extraordinary history. One of the projects I am particularly proud of this year is the documentary produced by TVMonaco, which retraces six decades of the Festival’s evolution and its close relationship with the television industry. Through archive footage and interviews, it highlights some of the remarkable personalities who have helped shape both the Festival and television itself. In the documentary, we hear reflections from figures such as Jerry Bruckheimer, Dick Wolf, Michel Drucker, Alexia Laroche-Joubert, Dominique Farrugia, Marie-France Brière and Rola Bauer, all of whom represent different chapters of television history. What makes me particularly proud is that the Festival has never been simply a celebration of fame. It has always been a place where creative talent, industry leaders and audiences can come together around a shared passion for storytelling. That spirit remains just as relevant today as it was in 1961. title: Idris Elba Says Bond Rumors Were ‘Never Legit’ and ‘Not Realistic’: Audiences ‘Won’t Go for a Black Male Playing Bond’
full_text: Jun 8, 2026 2:29am PT Idris Elba Says Bond Rumors Were ‘Never Legit’ and ‘Not Realistic’: Audiences ‘Won’t Go for a Black Male Playing Bond’ By Ellise Shafer Plus Icon Ellise Shafer Latest Idris Elba Says Bond Rumors Were ‘Never Legit’ and ‘Not Realistic’: Audiences ‘Won’t Go for a Black Male Playing Bond’ 3 hours ago ‘Toy Story 5’ VFX Supervisor Has Been Keeping Taylor Swift Song a Secret Since February: ‘We Had to Create a Decoy Version of the Film’ Without It 3 days ago Piers Morgan Says SXSW London ‘Could Have Stood Up’ More for Cenk Uygur After He Was Banned From Entering U.K.: ‘I’m a Bit Disappointed’ 4 days ago See All Getty Images Idris Elba is putting those James Bond rumors to bed. The 53-year-old acclaimed actor — who tends to play heroic characters and currently stars in “Masters of the Universe” alongside Nicholas Galitzine — has been tipped to take over the 007 role since before Daniel Craig’s departure in 2021. But in a new cover interview with British GQ , Elba says the rumors were “never legit.” Related Stories John Lithgow Play 'Giant' Recoups $5.6 Million Broadway Investment Tony Awards 2026: 'Death of a Salesman' Leads With Six Wins, as 'Ragtime,' 'Schmigadoon!' and 'Liberation' Also Take Top Honors “I’ve always felt that it’s not a realistic thing,” he told British GQ. “James Bond was written how he was written for a reason. But I was complimented by it. And also, I think, in realistic terms, some markets just don’t go for that. Bond is big all over the world. And [audiences] won’t [all] go for a Black male, an African male, playing Bond. That’s not what they like in their culture. Period.” Popular on Variety But he also said he doesn’t necessarily think that Bond should change. “Bond is so unrealistic, so a hint of reality is good, but let’s not try and make it woke,” Elba continued. “I think you’ve got to be pure to what it is: escapism. Don’t try and answer the world’s taste. Just be Bond.” The rumor mill as to who will be the next Bond has been heating up as auditions have officially started. Prolific casting director Nina Gold (“Game of Thrones,” “The Crown”) is leading the charge for Amazon MGM Studios, who took creative control of the franchise last year. The next Bond movie is set to be directed by “Dune” helmer Denis Villeneuve, and Variety has heard that he and the studio are looking for someone on the younger side to step into Bond’s shoes. Tom Francis, the 26-year-old British actor best known for his Olivier-winning role opposite Nicole Scherzinger in the stage musical “Sunset Boulevard,” has already auditioned, a source told Variety last month . Other stars heavily rumored to be in the mix include Jacob Elordi, Aaron Taylor-Johnson and Callum Turner. title: Maggie Gyllenhaal & Jesse Eisenberg To Be Honored At Karlovy Vary

Gyllenhaal will be handed the award at the festival’s opening ceremony. She will also screen The Bride! as part of the celebrations. Eisenberg will receive his award later in the festival, and he will present The Double , directed by Richard Ayoade. Eisenberg stars in the film alongside Mia Wasikowska. “Maggie Gyllenhaal and Jesse Eisenberg are distinguished by a certain ‘trans-Atlantic’ sensitivity,” Karlovy Vary Artistic Director Karel Och said in a statement. Sets HBO Max Premiere Date “Their work as actors and directors has the ability to connect the New World and Europe while drawing on the most essential elements of both traditions. The humor of New York intellectuals is combined with wisdom, talent, and an original way of seeing the world.” Watch on Deadline This year’s edition marks Karlovy Vary’s 60th anniversary. 12 titles have been set for the Festival’s Crystal Globe Competition. Among those titles is Hijamat, the latest feature from Iranian filmmaker Nader Saeivar. Saeivar is best known for his long-term collaboration with Jafar Panahi. The pair have written two films together, including last year’s Palme d’Or winner It Was Just an Accident . Check out the full list of competition titles here . The New Yorker film critic Justin Chang will serve on the jury of this year’s Crystal Globe Competition alongside filmmaker Amanda Nell Eu, Czech sound designer, producer, and composer Pavel Rejholec, producer Nadia Turincev, and filmmaker Eskil Vogt. title: Hugh Laurie Issues Waspish Retort To Viral Critique Of ‘House’ Having “Same Narrative Every Episode”

Janet Murray, a British journalist, went viral on X/Twitter over the weekend when she penned a review of House , in which she argued that the medical drama has the “same narrative every episode.” “Patient has mysterious illness. Hugh Laurie (House) gets diagnosis wrong. Patient nearly dies. Hugh Laurie gets diagnosis wrong again. Gets threatened with being fired. Patient nearly dies again,” she said. Gets diagnosis right. Doesn’t get fired. Eight seasons of this?” Watch on Deadline Laurie was not impressed. He responded: “Thanks for your critique, Janet. We actually tried a couple of episodes where House (Hugh Laurie) (please put the brackets in the right place) gets it right first time, but they were only 6 minutes long. NBC weren’t happy. Then we tried some where House never gets it right and the patient dies. The audience wasn’t happy.” The actor continued: “One could apply your trenchant analysis to other art forms: JS Bach wrote 30 Goldberg variations on the same chord structure; Frida Kahlo painted 50 portraits of herself; Henry Moore, what?? “The point is, or was, variations on a theme; if all you see is hospital, medical blah blah, then it wasn’t meant for you. title: Maggie Gyllenhaal, Jesse Eisenberg to Receive Karlovy Vary Honors
full_text: × Skip to main content By Georg Szalai Plus Icon Georg Szalai Global Business Editor View All June 8, 2026 2:00am Maggie Gyllenhall Courtesy of Gareth Cattermole/Getty Images Karlovy Vary is rolling out the red carpet for Maggie Gyllenhaal ( The Lost Daughter , Secretary , The Honourable Woman ) and Jesse Eisenberg ( The Social Network , A Real Pain , When You Finish Saving the World ) this year. At its 60th anniversary edition next month, the Karlovy Vary International Film Festival will present its President’s Award to the “two distinctive personalities who have significantly shaped contemporary world cinema,” organizers said on Monday. “In their work, director, screenwriter and actress Maggie Gyllenhaal and the multifaceted actor, director, screenwriter and author Jesse Eisenberg transition smoothly between charismatic performances and bold auteur filmmaking.” Related Stories Movies Tamil Blockbuster 'Karuppu' Set for Global Streaming Launch on Amazon Prime Video Movies 'Lesbian Lines' Doc Shines a Light on Irish History Not Taught in School - and the Power of Listening Said KVIFF executive director Kryštof Mucha: “We at the festival are glad to be able to welcome two creative individuals whose work as actors, screenwriters, and directors is representative of contemporary trends in cinema and whose films are an intense experience for audiences.” Jesse Eisenberg Courtesy of KVIFF Gyllenhaal will receive her honor during the opening ceremony of the 60th edition of KVIFF on Friday, July 3. At the fest, she will also present her film The Bride! , which she wrote, directed and produced. The “imaginative continuation of the classic Frankenstein story, influenced by both the 1935 film Bride of Frankenstein and Mary Shelley’s original masterpiece,” the movie stars Jessie Buckley, Christian Bale, Annette Bening, Penélope Cruz, Peter Sarsgaard and Jake Gyllenhaal. Also receiving KVIFF’s President’s Award will be Eisenberg, whose upcoming film as a writer and director, The Debut , stars Julianne Moore and Paul Giamatti. Eisenberg also co-stars in and wrote the music and lyrics for the film’s musical-within-the-movie. The movie will be released in the fall by A24. In his honor, the Czech festival will screen The Double , directed by Richard Ayoade, in which Eisenberg plays the lead role. “Maggie Gyllenhaal and Jesse Eisenberg are distinguished by a certain ‘trans-Atlantic’ sensitivity,” said KVIFF artistic director Karel Och. “Their work as actors and directors has the ability to connect the New World and Europe while drawing on the most essential elements of both traditions. The humor of New York intellectuals is combined with wisdom, talent, and an original way of seeing the world.” THR

title: Jamie Oliver Group Teaming With Baby Teeth For Microdrama & Wider Pact As Celebrity Chef’s Company Shifts Focus To IP
full_text: Continue to Deadline SKIP AD You will be redirected back to your article in seconds Skip to main content Dismiss News Banner Breaking News Tony Awards Winners: 'Schmigadoon!' Best Musical, 'Liberation' Best Play; John Lithgow, Lesley Manville, 'Ragtime' Duo Top Acting Honors Read the full story By Jesse Whittock Jesse Whittock International TV Co-Editor @WhittockTweets More Stories By Jesse ‘Heated Rivalry’ Execs Giving Jacob Tierney “The Time He Needs To Make Another Beautiful Season” Of The Ice-Hockey Drama ‘House Of The Dragon’s Steve Toussaint Promises Huge Sea Battle Opening Season 3 Will Be “Mayhem” – SXSW London ‘Meatballs’ TV Series Starring Robbie G.K., Mark-Paul Gosselaar Comedy ‘Bulges’ & ‘Littlest Hobo’ Reboot From Seth Rogen & Evan Goldberg On Bell Slate View All June 8, 2026 1:30am 'Jamie's 20 Minute Meals' Channel 4/Jamie Oliver Group EXCLUSIVE: The Jamie Oliver Group is developing a microdrama series with creative company Baby Teeth , as a strategic partnership between the two companies is unveiled amid a rethink at the campaigning celebrity chef’s media business. Details of the vertical video production are broadly under wraps, but it is in pre-production and still begin shooting in the UK this month, we hear. Jamie Oliver Group and Baby Teeth are working with a global consumer tech brand on the show, and an official announcement is expected soon. Baby Teeth, a UK-based creative company that launched last year, has quietly been working with Jamie Oliver Group as a strategic partner for some time on brand partnerships and special projects. Related Stories News Gabriela Tafur's Microdrama Platform Attracts Jeffrey Katzenberg's WndrCo To $5.5M Seed Funding Round News Sunset Las Palmas Studios Launching Sets Designed For Vertical Video Shoots At the same time, Jamie Oliver Group has moved Alison Corfield from Head of Social Impact & Sustainability to a new role as Brand Director, a sign of the 20 Minute Meals presenter’s new focus. Going forwards, Jamie Oliver Group will focus to formats, IP and creative output. Watch on Deadline “I’m doubling down on my lifelong mission to make good food accessible to everyone, but as we all know, the media landscape has changed beyond all recognition over the last few years so I’m looking for fresh storytelling and new digital formats to connect with people where they are right now,” said Oliver. “That’s why I’m so excited to be working with the creative powerhouse at Baby Teeth together with Alison Corfield leading the charge as Brand Director. She knows how to weave our social purpose into exciting and impactful brand partnerships and the brilliant creative minds at Baby Teeth give us that extra edge to tell those stories in unexpected, inspiring ways.” The changes comes after the company was forced to cut 25 of its 126 of its staff in December following a company review. A Jamie Oliver Group spokesperson told Deadline today: “The decision was one that was not taken lightly. Like many companies, the group looked at ways of working and structure to ensure we could continue on our mission to connect people around the world to food.” Baby Teeth – founded by former Gordon Ramsay brand chief Rebecca Lewis, former Channel 4 and Conde Nast exec Lynsey Atkin and Chris Watling – has been supporting Corfield on the development of new partnerships for the Jamie Oliver brand, especially in health, technology, travel and family. Corfield is best known for spearheading many of Oliver’s food campaigns such as #AdEnough, which resulted in the introduction of a junk food advertising watershed. “There are very few people who can genuinely move culture, influence policy, inspire businesses and still make millions of people feel excited about cooking dinner on a Tuesday night. Jamie can,” said Corfield, one of his most trusted lieutenants. “Having spent the last eight years working alongside him on campaigns that have changed laws, shifted conversations and improved lives, I know the scale of what’s possible when you combine his creativity, relentless optimism and deep belief that good food should be for everyone. “Food has never been more talked about, but the need for practical, positive change has never been greater. This next chapter is about unlocking the full power of Jamie’s brand, bringing together brilliant partners, new ideas and global platforms to help more people, in more places, live healthier, happier lives.” Baby Teeth launched with a plans to work with film and TV producers and decision makers to create new IP, as companies search for cost-efficient and creative ways to finance ideas. The Jamie Oliver Group pact is an example of the plan in action, with the chef’s catalog of IP on the table to be repurposed into new formats along with format development. “The most exciting talents in the world are asking a different question these days,” said Lewis. “Rather than just who can broker a deal, they want partners who can help them build something that’s not only relevant for today’s landscape but that endures beyond a launch moment. Jamie’s catalogue, his credibility, and his connection to audiences across generations make for an extraordinary, and valuable foundation to build from. “The work we’ve been doing with Alison and the team goes far beyond endorsement deals; it’s about finding brands that are strategically and creatively aligned with Jamie’s brand and business goals and ensuring that what comes out of the partnership is additive for Jamie, for the brand and most of all for the audience. “Jamie has been at the forefront of food and media ever since he appeared on our TV screens with The Naked Chef – pushing boundaries, reimagining formats, and doing things differently. We’re incredibly proud to build on that spirit as we help shape the next exciting chapter of the Jamie Oliver brand. Alongside the new direction for Jamie Oliver Group, celebrity chief Oliver has revived his Jamie’s Italian restaurant business six years after it went out of business. title: Tamil Blockbuster ‘Karuppu’ Set for Global Streaming Launch on Amazon Prime Video
full_text: × Skip to main content By Patrick Brzeski Plus Icon Patrick Brzeski Asia Bureau Chief View All June 8, 2026 1:27am Suriya in 'Karuppu' Dream Warrior Pictures Amazon Prime Video has set June 12 for the worldwide streaming premiere of Karuppu , the Tamil fantasy-action hit that has become the Indian industry’s top-grossing Tamil release of 2026 so far. Directed by RJ Balaji, Karuppu mixes mythology, courtroom drama and mass-action spectacle, with Tamil star Suriya playing the folk guardian deity Vettai Karuppu, who takes human form as a lawyer to take on a corrupt legal system preying on the poor. The cast also includes Trisha Krishnan and Indrans, with music by Sai Abhyankkar. On Prime Video , the film will stream in its original Tamil plus Hindi, Malayalam and Kannada dubs and a Telugu cut retitled Veerabhadrudu , across more than 200 countries and territories. The streaming bow comes about four weeks after the theatrical release and slots into Prime Video’s lineup ahead of Amazon’s Prime Day sales event in India in July. Related Stories Movies 'Lesbian Lines' Doc Shines a Light on Irish History Not Taught in School - and the Power of Listening Movies Iran Court Upholds Prison Sentence, Travel Ban for Jafar Panahi “I am truly humbled by the immense love and appreciation Karuppu has received at the box office,” Suriya said in a statement. Prime Video was previously the streaming home for the actor’s acclaimed Jai Bhim and Soorarai Pottru . Indian grosses aren’t officially audited, but local box office reports put Karuppu ‘s worldwide total above ₹300 crore (about $35 million) since its May 15 release — enough to make it Suriya’s biggest title ever and one of only about 10 Tamil films to cross that mark. His previous career best, the 2013 sequel Singam 2 , topped out around ₹120 crore, according to reports. Producer S R Prabhu says the film “celebrates the spirit of rooted storytelling while delivering the scale and emotion of a truly commercial entertainer,” and predicts it will “enjoy the same blockbuster success on streaming that it enjoyed in theaters.” Added Manish Menghani, director & head of content licensing, Prime Video, India: “Prime Video has been the preferred home to some of Suriya’s most acclaimed and impactful movies. With Karuppu , we continue this association, delivering larger-than-life storytelling, high-octane action, and intense performances from powerhouse talent.” THR

title: ‘Lesbian Lines’ Doc Shines a Light on Irish History Not Taught in School – and the Power of Listening
full_text: × Skip to main content By Georg Szalai Plus Icon Georg Szalai Global Business Editor View All June 7, 2026 11:55pm 'Lesbian Lines' Courtesy of Keeper Pictures In 1979, a small community of Irish lesbians established a network of underground telephone helplines to provide a lifeline for people experiencing isolation, abuse and/or profound loneliness. After all, homosexuality faced intense social and legal hostility in a conservative Ireland. The feature documentary Lesbian Lines , which was directed by Cara Holmes ( Notes From Sheepland ) will world premiere in the international competition lineup of Sheffield DocFest on Thursday, June 11, now brings an intimate portrait of these help- and life-lines to the big screen. Related Stories Movies Iran Court Upholds Prison Sentence, Travel Ban for Jafar Panahi Movies Kaouther Ben Hania on Funding Paradoxes and How "Every Movie Is Political" Audiences get to meet original helpline volunteers, watch actors bring to life this so far undocumented part of history through dramatized sequences and relive or discover archival material. The resulting cinematic mix is as moving as it is joyful. The phone volunteers logged every call, and Lesbian Lines even shows us these handwritten notes, along with old telephones. With cinematography by Aidan Gault and editing by Colin Campbell and Holmes, Lesbian Lines tells a story of community, connection and the power of listening. Holmes actually started off in editing, saying: “When I’m not directing, I do edit feature documentaries.” And she tells THR : “I feel my role is very fluid as a filmmaker, so I like to not keep things too defined. I like the openness of being able to do a few different things.” Lesbian Lines comes from executive producers Katie Holly and Yvonne Donohoe of Keeper Pictures , Niamh Fagan of Screen Ireland, and Sally Roden and Grainne McAleer of Irish broadcaster RTÉ . The producer is Evan Horan of Keeper Pictures, who is also handling sales. Holmes talked to THR about the challenges and pleasures of documenting a period of history that suddenly doesn’t feel quite like a distant past anymore, the universal and timely themes the film explores and why it was key for Lesbian Lines to feature not just darkness but also joy. Why did you want to tell the story of Lesbian Lines ? Sometimes there’s so much history and there is so much behind it. This absolutely was a helpline, and many of the women I spoke to told me that you can’t underestimate the power of making a simple phone call and, on the other side, having someone answering the phone. It’s about that really simple act of having someone listen to you, having someone support you, having someone invite you into a community, feeling this belonging. It’s about all of those things that we need as human beings. When you live in a society that really was just piling on lots of shame and wanting to push you out, all of those things are particularly important. For me, the personal motivation was that this history isn’t in our school books. Why are we learning one type of history but not this whole other side of queer history, women’s history. I call these women my heroes because they paved the way for younger generations, including myself, to live in a freer society. These are women to be worshipped. When did you start work on it? I frame it around COVID, because that’s when I was doing a lot of the research, and there were so many history talks online. It was great to see these old-school telephones. Where did you get those for the film and how key to the storytelling did you feel they were? It was funny because a lot of the volunteers kept a lot of the phones, because they knew how important this work was in their lives. It was this idea of the landline and one connecting cable. We did a bit of sourcing, but I had a couple [of phones], because I’m old enough. It wasn’t too difficult, and it is a really iconic piece of machinery now. It’s just the simplicity that you had the handset and the dial. A lot of the women were talking about just finding that privacy, for example in the telephone box if that was not really there at home. A lot of the women had to go outside to the local telephone box and were really worried that somebody was standing outside listening in to the conversation. If they had a phone in their house, they moved rooms or waited to make sure nobody was there. One of the themes that I felt the doc keep suggesting that I walked away with is the idea that you have to look at the past to enable the presence and the future. Can you talk about that a bit? A big challenge for us was: How do you make a film about this kind of queer history when conventional archives fail you, when there are no real archives to tell the story? So a big part of it for us was to bring in younger generations to demonstrate what the women were talking about. Ny bringing in these younger queer actors, they were learning on the job as well as they were reading the scripts and stories and meeting the older women. That was key for me. While it’s absolutely fantastic to document these stories. It’s all about learning and giving younger generations the confidence to know what came before them. Knowing what came before you just propels you into having confidence. We talk a lot about shame of the past. There is still that shame around, but I think when you see that there are older people, 30, 40, 50 years ago, doing all this work and bringing it to you, then you learn about it and can carry it forward. And you see yourself as part of which generation? I very much see myself in that middle generation. I’m some kind of weird transmitter. And it was an absolute privilege for me to listen to these women’s stories. A lot of younger people don’t want to talk on the phone anymore, but I spent my entire COVID phoning up these older women to hear their stories, so I could personally learn and bring all of that stuff into the film. It’s kind of like a lesbian transmission, really, flowing down the generations. And I have really no doubt that by knowing these women existed, knowing their stories, listening to them, I have more confidence in myself going out into the world with this breadth of knowledge. How much do you think things have improved for lesbians in Ireland and beyond? It was definitely a harder country to live in back in the ’80s – 100 percent. It was harder than it is now for some people. For trans people, it’s still quite difficult. I think in the U.K., at the moment, it seems much more problematic than in Ireland for trans people when it comes to being able to live their lives in the way that they want. Just like women were treated as lesbian women back in the ’80s, it’s exactly the same arguments that trans people are hearing now. It is definitely a case of history repeating itself and fear mongering and all of those things coming back into the ether. As these women in our film were talking about the historical elements, the parallels were kind of depressing, because you are wondering: “Have we not moved on to just let people live and be who they want to be, confidently and freely?” I felt the pain and challenges in Lesbian Lines, but also the positive and the joy, which left me hopeful. Can you explain how you approached the tone of the doc? For the film, I just wanted to always bring it back to that human level from this abstract political thing. Even using the word “lesbian,” is giving people that confidence to use those words as well and claim those identities in a positive way. It’s massive, huge, to be able to just live in your own identity confidently. And I felt that the phone line specifically was a way to get into that history, but always bring it back to the idea of a phone call and how that can just transform people’s lives. While things were bad, the women in the film were super clear to me that they didn’t want to be in a film that didn’t have queer joy in it. Because while it was hard and they had to go through a lot of stuff, they had an awful lot of fun, too, and they want younger people to see that as well. They had lives, they were partying, they were doing all this kind of stuff, but it was underground and grassroots. So, it was important to strike that balance between the hardships and the queer joy. Have you considered making all the insight you collected for Lesbian Lines available in some form to a museum or archive or so? You’re doing so much research for a film. This was a six-year history project, and you just do a deep dive into it. So, I really feel like I have a doctorate in Lesbian Lines studies. I’ve been thinking an awful lot about it. The film is one thing and great to have, but there is so much more material – from the women in the film alone. I have two to three hours of material from each one, and I called at least 50 or 60 women during the research period. In Ireland, we have the Irish Queer Archive, which is great, but it’s hard enough to access through the national library. So if someone came to me and said, “I’m going to offer you a website and a space to put all this material,” I would be running to them, because it’s too precious for people not to hear it – with consent from the women, of course. Yeah, I think there should be whole library sections dedicated to this. THR

title: Tony Awards Analysis: A Strange Broadway Season Ends with Recognition for Both Hollywood Heavyweights and Theater Troupers
full_text: × Skip to main content By Scott Feinberg Plus Icon Scott Feinberg Executive Editor of Awards ScottFeinberg Follow Sign Up View All June 7, 2026 11:53pm From left: John Lithgow, Laurie Metcalf, Joshua Henry and Shoshana Bean Theo Wargo/Getty Images for Tony Awards Productions; Theo Wargo/Getty Images for Tony Awards Productions; Theo Wargo/Getty Images for Tony Awards Productions; Michele Crowe/CBS via Getty Images The 2025-2026 Broadway season — the highest-grossing in history at $1.9 billion, even though it included only six new musicals, which are usually what drive ticket sales — came to an end on Sunday night with the 79th Tony Awards at Radio City Music Hall in New York. The verdict of the 831 voters? 10 shows were worthy of recognition in at least one category: Becky Shaw , Cats: The Jellicle Ball , Death of a Salesman , Giant , Fallen Angels , Liberation , The Lost Boys , Oedipus , Ragtime and Schmigadoon! . Best musical, the one Tony that tends to result in a meaningful box-office boost for its winner, went to Schmigadoon! , a Lorne Michaels-backed sendup of golden age Broadway musicals, over The Lost Boys , a show that appealed to a younger demo. It was a battle of screen adaptations — Schmigadoon! was inspired by the Apple TV series of the same name, while The Lost Boys is a musical spin on the 1987 film of the same name — which shared the distinction of most Tony noms of any show this season , 12. Related Stories Lifestyle Tonys Snubs: 'Lost Boys' Actor Ali Louis Bourzgui Scores Surprise Win Over André De Shields as 'Rocky Horror,' 'Two Strangers,' 'Titaníque' Shut Out Lifestyle 'Schmigadoon!' Wins Best Musical at 2026 Tony Awards as 'Liberation' Wins Best Play In what was seen as a similarly close contest, Lincoln Center’s production of Ragtime , the first on Broadway since the show first ran from 1998 through 2000, won best revival of a musical over Cats: The Jellicle Ball , an edgy reimagining of the polarizing show that ran on the Great White Way from 1982 through 2000. The remaining musical awards, which consider both new productions and revivals alongside each other, were spread fairly evenly between the aforementioned four shows. Best direction went to Cats (specifically to the team of Zhailon Levingston and Bill Rauch), as did best choreography and costume design (making Qween Jean the first openly trans person to win a Tony). Best actor and actress went to Ragtime ’s Joshua Henry and Caissie Levy, respectively, and the show was also recognized for best sound design. The Lost Boys claimed best featured actor and featured actress for, respectively, 26-year-old Ali Louis Bourzgui, who upset Cats’ André De Shields, and veteran trouper Shoshana Bean, as well as lighting design of a musical and scenic design of a musical. (The legendary De Shields, 80, won seven years ago for Hadestown .) And Schmigadoon! also took home best book, original score and orchestrations. (Its wins made Apple TV the fastest streamer to an EGOT , just six-and-a-half years after its launch.) Liberation , a memory play about 1970s feminism, which started off Broadway and was awarded the Pulitzer Prize for drama last month , was chosen for the best play Tony over Giant , a troubling portrait of children’s author Roald Dahl at the moment when he was first facing accusations of antisemitism — this despite the fact that the former closed back in February, while the latter is still running and a huge box office hit, having recouped its $5.6 million capitalization in just 10 weeks. Rather remarkably, Liberation scribe Bess Wohl became t he first female American playwright in 38 years to win the best play Tony , following the late Wendy Wasserstein, who was awarded it for The Heidi Chronicles . Oddly enough, though, that high honor was the only win of the night for Liberation , while Giant claimed its sole trophy for its lead actor John Lithgow, marking his third Tony win, 53 years after his first. Most of the awards for plays, though, went to a universally-acclaimed production of Arthur Miller’s “Great American Play,” Death of a Salesman . Its sixth remounting on Broadway since the 1949 original was recognized with best revival, direction (Joe Mantello’s third Tony for directing, after the play Take Me Out in 2003 and the musical Assassins in 2004), featured actress (Laurie Metcalf’s third win, all in the last decade, after A Doll’s House, Part 2 in 2017 and Three Tall Women in 2018), lighting design, scenic design and sound design. Indeed, no show of any genre was recognized with more Tonys this year than the six for Death of a Salesman . Alas, the show’s heartbreaking Willy Loman, Nathan Lane, was stopped short of a fourth Tony win by Lithgow, in what was probably the most closely-watched race of the night; and Biff Loman, 40-year-old Christopher Abbott, was upset by a fellow young thespian who is also known for screen work, 36-year-old Alden Ehrenreich for Becky Shaw , a revival of a dark comedy in which Ehrenreich portrays a truly toxic male . Ehrenreich’s win was the only one for Becky Shaw . This year’s two other shows with one-off wins were Oedipus , for which Lesley Manville won best actress in a play in recognition of her Broadway debut at age 70 (the show closed back in February); and Fallen Angels , the first Broadway revival in 70 years of a lesser-known Noel Coward comedy (it closed earlier on Sunday), which snagged best costume design for a play. Meanwhile, 14 Tony-nominated shows went home completely emptyhanded: Two Strangers (Carry a Cake Across New York) , The Balusters , Little Bear Ridge Road , Titaníque , Richard O’Brien’s The Rocky Horror Show , Every Brilliant Thing , Punch , Bug , Chess , Marjorie Prime , Waiting for Godot , Joe Turner’s Come and Gone , Dog Day Afternoon and The Fear of 13 . Fun fact: three of the six Tony-nominated performers who appeared on this year’s THR Tonys Roundtable — Bean, Henry and Lithgow — took home hardware. THR

title: Tonys Snubs: ‘Lost Boys’ Actor Ali Louis Bourzgui Scores Surprise Win Over André De Shields as ‘Rocky Horror,’ ‘Two Strangers,’ ‘Titaníque’ Shut Out
full_text: × Skip to main content By Hilary Lewis , Caitlin Huston June 7, 2026 10:29pm Ali Louis Bourzgui, Andre De Shields and Luke Evans in 'Richard O’Brien’s The Rocky Horror Show.' Getty Images Ragtime and Cats: The Jellicle Ball battled it out at the 2026 Tonys , leading to some unexpected wins and snubs. The musical revivals went into Sunday night’s awards ceremony with 11 and nine nominations respectively , and they ended the night with Ragtime in a slight lead with four Tonys to Cats: The Jellicle Ball ‘s three awards. Ragtime triumphed in the best musical revival category , arguably the closest race of the night. However, Zhailon Levingston and Bill Rauch, who directed Cats: The Jellicle Ball , won best direction of a musical over Ragtime director Lear deBessonet, triumphing in a close race. Related Stories Lifestyle 'Schmigadoon!' Wins Best Musical at 2026 Tony Awards as 'Liberation' Wins Best Play Lifestyle Tony Awards 2026: 'Book of Mormon' Stars Josh Gad and Andrew Rannells Reprise Their Roles Another Cats snub and surprise win came in the featured actor in a musical race. Theater legend André De Shields was seen as having an edge in the category for his role as Old Deuteronomy, a literally show-stopping performance, but instead Ali Louis Bourzgui was the surprise winner for his role as The Lost Boys ‘ charismatic vampire leader. Bourzgui delivered a powerful speech, in which he spoke out against fascists and billionaires and in support of Arab artists and Palestinians. Another notable surprise and snub came in the featured actor in a play category as Becky Shaw ‘s Alden Ehrenreich beat out Death of a Salesman ‘s Christopher Abbott in what was another close race. Death of a Salesman also suffered a rare defeat in the lead actor in a play category, when star Nathan Lane lost to Giant ‘s John Lithgow, though Lithgow had been widely viewed as the winner, particularly after he won the Olivier Award for the same role last year. Still, Lane led a strong campaign and was praised for his performance. A number of shows that went into the night with multiple Tony Awards nominations were completely shut out: Nine-time nominee Richard O’Brien’s The Rocky Horror Show , eight-time nominee Two Strangers (Carry a Cake Across New York) and four-time nominee Titaníque . THR

title: John Oliver Jokes CBS News Fired Scott Pelley For “The Crime Of Being Too Cool In A Meeting”
full_text: Continue to Deadline SKIP AD You will be redirected back to your article in seconds Skip to main content Dismiss News Banner Breaking News Tony Awards Winners: 'Schmigadoon!' Best Musical, 'Liberation' Best Play; John Lithgow, Lesley Manville, 'Ragtime' Duo Top Acting Honors Read the full story By Natalie Oganesyan Natalie Oganesyan Weekend Editor @nat_oganesyan More Stories By Natalile John Oliver Jokes CBS News Fired Scott Pelley For “The Crime Of Being Too Cool In A Meeting” John C. Reilly Remembers Trying To Convince Leonardo DiCaprio To Drop Out Of ‘Titanic’ Role For ‘Boogie Nights’: “It’s About A Boat That Sinks” Marlon Wayans Reveals Melissa Joan Hart Was Supposed To Play Anna Faris’ Role In ‘Scary Movie’ View All June 7, 2026 8:20pm John Oliver / Scott Pelley YouTube / Getty Images John Oliver wasted no time getting into the headlines during the evening’s Last Week Tonight episode, addressing one of the big pieces of news over the weekend: the continuing fallout of CBS News ‘ ousting of longtime correspondent Scott Pelley . “CBS News fired Scott Pelley for the crime of: being too cool in a meeting,” the comic quipped, referencing the 37-year veteran’s unceremonious dismissal following his tense verbal confrontation with editor-in-chief Bari Weiss and newly minted 60 Minutes executive producer Nick Bilton. During the meeting, which took place last Monday when Bilton was introducing himself to network staffers, the 51-time Emmy-winning broadcast journalist grilled the EP and The Free Press founder about their qualifications, adding that the latter is “murdering” the stalwart newsmagazine. Related Stories News FCC Chair Brendan Carr Blasts Scott Pelley As "Completely Out Of Touch" After Journalist Says His '60 Minutes' Firing Came As A Surprise News Scott Pelley, In First Interview Since '60 Minutes' Firing, Accuses CBS News Brass Of "Subtle Political Bias," Says They "Don't Know What They're Doing" Earlier this weekend, Pelley sat down with The New York Times to tell his side of the story, opening up in an inaugural interview following his dismissal. He told the publication that being fired was “the furthest thing from my mind” during the Tuesday emergency meeting he had with leadership. “So I’m thinking that the meeting’s going to carry on. We’re going to have a long conversation. Very quickly after the meeting began, [CBS News president] Tom Cibrowski said, this conversation is over. I was stunned,” he recalled. Watch on Deadline On Sunday, FCC chairman Brendan Carr took to social media to slam Pelley as “completely out of touch,” maintaining that “You could not get away with that behavior at any run of the mill job.
